title: Pluripotency markers staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6174h","text":"This protocol describes the standard procedure for staining pluripotency markers, e.g. OCT4, SSEA4, alkaline phosphatase, and etc. on human pluripotent stem cells (hPSCs). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"29qda","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"egqj2","text":"Protocol overview","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4u35j","text":"A. All described procedures have been tested and work equally well for hiPSCs and hESCs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Wash cells once with PBS
Fix cells with 4% PFA at Room temperature for 00:15:00 You should not fix cells if staining for cell surface proteins
Wash cells twice with PBS, incubate 00:05:00 in between washes at Room temperature
Incubate in 3% Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) for 01:00:00 at Room temperature
Incubate with primary antibody (1:200) in 3% BSA at 4 °C Overnight
Wash three times with PBS, incubate 00:05:00 in between washes at Room temperature
Incubate with secondary antibody (1:1,000) in 3% BSA at Room temperature for 01:00:00 in the dark and in a humidified chamber.
Wash once with PBS
Incubate with 0.1 µg/ml DAPI for 00:05:00 at Room temperature
Wash twice with PBS, incubate 00:05:00 in between washes at Room temperature
Image cells, seal the plate with parafilm, and wrap with foil for longer storage at 4 °C . Florescence is usually stable for several weeks.
Phosphate buffer saline, PBS, pH 7.4 A B 1 NaCl 137 mM 2 KCl 2.7 mM 3 Na2HPO4 10 mM 4 KH2PO4 1.8 mM 5 CaCl2•2H2O 1 mM 6 MgCl2•6H2O 0.5 mM
Incubate in PBST for 00:30:00 at Room temperature to permeabilize cell membrane
PBST, pH 7.4 A B 1 PBS 1x 2 Triton-X100 0.3%
4% PFA, pH 7.4 A B 1 PBS 500 ml 2 PFA 20 g 3 NaOH Adjust pH to 7.4 Final volume: 500 ml
3% Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) A B 1 PBS 500 ml 2 BSA 15 g
Wash once with PBS
Fix cells with cold 4% PFA, 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Wash twice with PBS
Incubate cells with 100 mM Tris, pH 9.5, 00:10:00 at Room temperature

Add NBT/BCIP substrate in 100 mM Tris, and let reaction develop at Room temperature , avoiding light until color becomes clearly apparent in control cells. We have used NBT/BCIP substrate from two different vendors, which can be found in the materials list. The formulation for each are as follows: A B C 1 NBT/BCIP substrate Vector 1 drop of each reagent in 6 ml 2 NBT/BCIP substrate Roche 100 µl to 10 ml
Wash in PBS and keep in PBS (violet product is soluble in organic solvent).
100 mM Tris, pH 9.5 A B 1 Tris 6.57g 2 HCl Adjust pH to 9.5 Final volume: 500 ml
creator: Hanqin Li
